Title: Investigate Shariah-compliant PLS contract model (musharakah/mudharabah) for MVP implementation
Description: R07 requires a profit-loss sharing model compliant with AAOIFI/IFSB guidance. The team lacks detailed knowledge of the exact profit-split rules, loss allocation, and required audit trails. This Spike will research authoritative sources (Malaysia SC, AAOIFI), consult with the Shariah Board (if available), and propose a simplified but compliant model suitable for the 12-week MVP.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given research into AAOIFI and Malaysia SC guidelines, when the findings are compiled, then a documented summary of mandatory PLS elements is produced.
? Given the mandatory elements, when reviewed with the Shariah Board (or liaison), then a simplified PLS model for MVP is agreed.
? Given the agreed model, when documented in an ADR, then the ADR includes profit-split formulas, loss allocation rules, and data fields to be recorded.
Dependencies: Access to Shariah Board or qualified Islamic finance expert
Notes:
- add an explicit profit-split formula to ADR-2026-001, such as:
- investor_profit = distributable_profit ? rabb_al_mal_ratio
- mudarib_profit = distributable_profit ? mudarib_ratio
- attach or reference the actual PBI-001 findings/summary document

Title: Define hybrid blockchain anchoring approach for immutable audit trail
Description: R05 requires an immutable audit trail for procure-to-pay events. The project constraints (Section 4) mandate a centralized-first or hybrid architecture, not a full blockchain rollout. This Spike will investigate lightweight methods to achieve immutability and auditability, such as: hashing events and storing proofs on a permissioned blockchain (or public testnet), using a trusted timestamping authority, or storing hashes in a tamper-evident database (e.g., Amazon QLDB). The goal is to select the simplest approach that meets audit requirements and can be implemented within 12 weeks.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given the project constraints (Section 4.2), when at least three anchoring options are evaluated, then a comparison matrix is produced (cost, complexity, immutability guarantees).
? Given the comparison, when reviewed with the Tech Lead and PO, then a recommended approach is selected.
? Given the selected approach, when documented in an ADR, then the ADR includes architecture diagram, data flow, and evidence of a working proof-of-concept (e.g., script that hashes an event and stores it on a test network).
Dependencies: Access to test blockchain network (e.g., Hyperledger Fabric test environment or public testnet)
Notes:
- add an architecture diagram to ADR-2026-002
- add a short data-flow section
- add a direct PoC evidence/reference section in the ADR

Title: Supplier onboarding with KYC/AML check
Description: As a supplier, I want to register on the platform and complete a KYC check so that I can participate in procure-to-pay activities.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a new supplier, when they submit registration with business details and documents, then a KYC record is created with status 'Pending'.
? Given a submitted KYC, when an admin approves it, then the supplier status becomes 'Verified'.
? Given a verified supplier, when they log in, then they see their verified badge.
Notes: Manual KYC approval in MVP; document upload only.

Title: Role-based access and user management
Description: As a platform admin, I want to assign roles (SME, Buyer, Bank) to users so that they see appropriate dashboards and permissions.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given an admin user, when they create a new user, they can select a role from SME, Buyer, Bank.
? Given a user with role SME, when they log in, they see only SME functions (e.g., submit invoice).
? Given a user with role Buyer, when they log in, they see procurement functions (create PO, approve delivery).
Notes: Simple role enum; no fine-grained permissions.

Title: Procure-to-pay workflow: Create and approve purchase order
Description: As a buyer, I want to create a purchase order and have the supplier accept it, so that the procurement process begins.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a buyer, when they create a PO with supplier, items, and value, then the PO is stored with status 'Pending Supplier Acceptance'.
? Given a PO with status 'Pending', when the supplier views it and clicks Accept, then the PO status changes to 'Accepted'.
? Given an accepted PO, when the supplier delivers goods, they can mark it as 'Delivered'.
Notes: Simple workflow without escrow yet.

Title: Invoice submission and approval
Description: As a supplier, I want to submit an invoice for a delivered PO so that I can get paid.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a PO marked 'Delivered', when the supplier uploads an invoice, then the invoice is created with status 'Pending Buyer Approval'.
? Given an invoice, when the buyer approves it, then the invoice status changes to 'Approved'.
? Given an approved invoice, the system records the amount due.
Notes: No payment execution in MVP; just status.

Title: Basic PLS profit/loss calculation and recording
Description: As a bank, I want to record a PLS contract and compute profit/loss for a financing so that the Shariah-compliant distribution is tracked.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a PLS contract created with parameters (profit ratio, capital amounts), when an approved invoice has a manual settlement record posted, the system calculates profit share according to the ADR-2026-001 formula.
? Given a calculated profit, the system records it in the audit trail with contract ID, amounts, and timestamp.
Notes: Implementation based on ADR-2026-001.

Title: Immutable audit trail for procure-to-pay events
Description: As an auditor, I want every key event (PO, delivery, invoice, approval) to be recorded with a hash anchored to an immutable store so that the log cannot be tampered with.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given any PO creation, when it is saved, the system generates a hash of the event data and stores it in the audit log with timestamp.
? Given a delivery event, when recorded, its hash is anchored using the method defined in ADR-2026-002.
? Given an auditor request, the system can export a signed bundle of events with their hashes for verification.
Notes:
Implements hybrid anchoring as per spike.
MVP export signature is application-level signing; external TSA/WORM integration may be mocked or deferred if not required for Sprint 2 acceptance.

Title: Audit report export for regulators
Description: As a regulator, I want to request and receive an export of all relevant transactions for a given period so that I can perform compliance checks.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a regulator user, when they select a date range and request export, the system generates a CSV file with all events (POs, deliveries, invoices, PLS records) and their hashes.
? The export includes a digital signature to verify authenticity.
Notes: Simple export; no custom queries.

Title: Shariah governance workflow (manual approval)
Description: As a Shariah Board member, I want to review and approve new PLS products before they are used, so that compliance is ensured.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a new PLS contract template, when it is submitted for approval, a task is created for the Shariah Board.
? Given a Shariah Board member, they can view the contract details and mark it as 'Approved' or 'Rejected'.
? Approved contracts are recorded in the system with a reference to the approval.
Dependencies: Manual workflow; no on-chain signatures in MVP.

Title: Role-based dashboards for SMEs, Buyers, Banks
Description: As a user, I want a dashboard tailored to my role showing relevant information and actions.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given a logged-in SME, the dashboard shows their open POs, submitted invoices, and PLS contracts (if any).
? Given a buyer, the dashboard shows pending supplier acceptances, invoices to approve, and audit reports.
? Given a bank, the dashboard shows PLS contracts and profit/loss records.

Title: Access logging with non-repudiation
Description: Ensure all user actions that change state are logged with user identity and a cryptographic signature (or hash) to support non-repudiation.
AcceptanceCriteria:
? Given any state-changing API call (create PO, approve invoice), the system logs the user ID, timestamp, and a hash of the action.
? The log is stored in the audit trail and included in exports.

Title: Create PLS contract schema and persistence model
Description: Implement database schema/model for MVP PLS contracts based on ADR-2026-001, including party roles, capital amount, profit-sharing ratios, restrictions, review status, and versioning fields.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given ADR-2026-001 required fields, when the migration is applied, then the system can persist a PLS contract with contract_id, procurement_reference_id, party roles, capital_amount, profit ratios, restrictions, review status, and timestamps.
• Given an invalid contract payload, when required fields are missing, then persistence is rejected with validation errors.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007. Use restricted mudarabah MVP baseline from ADR-2026-001.

Title: Implement PLS contract validation and create/read API
Description: Implement backend API/service for a bank user to create and retrieve PLS contracts, enforcing MVP Shariah validation rules from ADR-2026-001.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given a valid PLS contract payload, when the bank submits it, then the contract is created and can be retrieved by ID.
• Given profit-sharing ratios that do not total 100%, when submitted, then the request is rejected.
• Given a contract containing prohibited guarantee fields or values, when submitted, then the request is rejected.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007. Enforce no guaranteed principal/profit for mudarabah.

Title: Add manual settlement record for approved invoices
Description: Add an MVP manual settlement record/status for approved invoices so that PLS calculation can be triggered without implementing payment execution.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given an approved invoice, when a bank or admin records settlement manually, then a settlement record is created with invoice_id, settled_amount, settled_at, and actor.
• Given an invoice not yet approved, when settlement is attempted, then the action is rejected.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007. This resolves the 'invoice settled' trigger gap in the current backlog.

Title: Implement PLS profit/loss calculation service
Description: Implement service logic to compute distributable profit/loss for an approved-and-settled financing using ADR-2026-001 formulas and MVP loss rules.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given a settled invoice linked to a valid PLS contract, when calculation is triggered, then distributable profit and each party share are computed according to ADR-2026-001.
• Given a mudarabah loss case without negligence override, when loss is recorded, then the loss is allocated to rabb_al_mal by default.
• Given invalid calculation inputs, when the service runs, then the system returns a clear validation error.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007. Formula source: ADR-2026-001.

Title: Persist PLS calculation result and emit audit event
Description: Store each completed PLS calculation result and emit a canonical audit event for the shared audit-trail pipeline.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given a successful PLS calculation, when it completes, then the result is persisted with contract_id, invoice_id, source amounts, result amounts, and timestamp.
• Given a persisted PLS calculation, when recording completes, then a canonical audit event is emitted for the audit pipeline.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007. Shared audit event format must match PBI-019.

Title: Add unit and integration tests for PLS calculations
Description: Add automated tests for PLS contract validation, settlement-triggered calculation, profit split, and default mudarabah loss handling.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given the PLS service test suite, when tests run, then the suite covers valid profit split, invalid ratio totals, prohibited guarantees, and default mudarabah loss allocation.
• Given an end-to-end contract + settlement flow, when executed in test, then a persisted PLS record is produced.
Notes: Parent: PBI-007.

Title: Define canonical audit event schema and hashing utility
Description: Define the shared canonical audit event payload and implement deterministic hashing utility for all state-changing procure-to-pay events.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given the same event payload content, when canonicalized and hashed multiple times, then the hash result is identical.
• Given an event payload, when hashed, then the stored audit record includes event_type, source_id, timestamp, actor, and event_hash.
Notes: Parent: PBI-008. Base implementation must follow ADR-2026-002.

Title: Hook audit events into PO delivery invoice approval and PLS flows
Description: Integrate audit event creation into key business flows: PO creation, supplier acceptance, delivery, invoice submission, invoice approval, settlement record, and PLS record.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given any supported state-changing event, when the action succeeds, then a canonical audit event is created and stored.
• Given PO creation, delivery, invoice submission, invoice approval, settlement, and PLS calculation, when each occurs, then each produces a distinct audit event.
Notes: Parent: PBI-008. Include PLS events for cross-story traceability.

Title: Implement evidence manifest and Merkle-root generation
Description: Implement daily evidence manifest generation from stored event hashes and compute a Merkle root as defined in ADR-2026-002.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given one or more stored audit events, when the manifest job runs, then a manifest containing event hashes and a Merkle root is generated.
• Given unchanged source events, when the manifest is regenerated, then the same Merkle root is produced for the same ordered set.
Notes: Parent: PBI-008. MVP may store bundle locally/file-system first if immutable-store integration is deferred.

Title: Implement audit export bundle and application signature
Description: Implement auditor export of events, hashes, manifest metadata, and an MVP application signature or detached signature file for verification.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given an auditor export request for a date range, when export is generated, then the bundle contains events, hashes, manifest metadata, and export timestamp.
• Given an exported bundle, when signature verification is performed with the application public key or verification metadata, then integrity/authenticity can be checked.
Notes: Parent: PBI-008. MVP signature may be application-level signing rather than external PKI.

Title: Add tests for deterministic hashing manifests and export verification
Description: Add automated tests for canonical hashing, manifest generation, Merkle-root reproducibility, and export verification.
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Given repeated hashing of equivalent event payloads, when tests run, then the resulting hashes are identical.
• Given a generated manifest, when verification runs, then the Merkle root matches the underlying event hashes.
• Given an exported bundle, when verification runs, then signature and manifest checks pass.
Notes: Parent: PBI-008.
